summary: ADA 2026 and Computex dominated the week. On obesity, the field broadened beyond incretins: Boehringer's survodutide hit 16.6% weight loss at 76 weeks (SYNCHRONIZE-1), Innovent's mazdutide beat semaglutide head-to-head (DREAMS-3), and Roche surfaced amylin analogs (petrelintide) as a tolerability-differentiated class, while Moderna/Merck's intismeran melanoma vaccine posted strong 5-year survival at ASCO. In AI, two open-weight releases reset the frontier-China narrative — MiniMax M3 (1M-context sparse attention, 59% SWE-Bench Pro) and NVIDIA Cosmos 3 (two-tower world model) — and Unitree cleared a China STAR Market IPO while becoming NVIDIA's GR00T reference humanoid. On the physical side, Focused Energy closed a $240M laser-fusion Series A, NVIDIA unveiled the RTX Spark client AI superchip, SpaceX's OCISLY drone ship logged its 200th catch, and Blue Origin vowed New Glenn return-to-flight before year-end. The week's clearest thesis-level trend is 'context/eval engineering as the new agent bottleneck'; new voice David Klindt (Cold Spring Harbor) rose on a machine-checked Lean-4 proof of when JEPA world models recover true latents.

highlights.title: MiniMax M3: open-weight, 1M-context sparse attention, 59% SWE-Bench Pro
highlights.detail: MiniMax released M3 on June 1, 2026 with a new MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) architecture giving a 1M-token context at ~1/20th the per-token compute of M2 (>9x prefill, >15x decode speedup), scoring 59.0% on SWE-Bench Pro (surpassing GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro) with native image/video input and computer use. Open weights + technical report committed within 10 days.

payload.claim: MiniMax M3 (June 1, 2026): open-weight model with 1M-token context via MiniMax Sparse Attention, 59.0% SWE-Bench Pro (beats GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro, approaches Opus 4.7), native multimodality and computer use; weights+report within 10 days.

highlights.title: Long-horizon autonomous agentic runs as a reasoning benchmark
highlights.detail: MiniMax documented M3 running fully autonomous multi-hour engineering loops: ~12h reproducing an ICLR 2025 paper (18 commits, 23 figures) and ~24h optimizing an FP8 GEMM CUDA kernel over 147 benchmark submissions / 1,959 tool calls, lifting Hopper FP8 peak utilization from 7.6% to 71.3%. Frames test-time compute as sustained autonomous iteration rather than single-turn chain-of-thought.

payload.name: Long-horizon autonomous agentic reasoning
payload.description: Evaluating reasoning models by their ability to run unattended for many hours through plan-execute-diagnose-iterate loops on real research and systems tasks, measured by submissions/tool-calls/commits and end-state performance rather than single-pass accuracy.
examples: MiniMax M3 reproducing an ICLR 2025 paper autonomously over ~12h (18 commits, 23 figures)
MiniMax M3 optimizing an FP8 GEMM kernel on NVIDIA Hopper over ~24h (147 submissions, 1,959 tool calls), 7.6%->71.3% peak utilization

highlights.title: NVIDIA Cosmos 3: two-tower MoT world model unifying reasoning, video gen, action
highlights.detail: NVIDIA released Cosmos 3 (May 31, 2026), an open omnimodal world-model family pairing an autoregressive VLM reasoner tower with a diffusion-based generator tower (initialized from Qwen3-VL) for physics-aware video, sound, and action generation. Ships Nano (16B) and Super (64B) checkpoints under OpenMDW-1.1, reporting open-source SOTA on R-Bench and leading Artificial Analysis text-to-image and image-to-video.

payload.claim: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 (May 31, 2026): open Mixture-of-Transformers world-model family unifying physical reasoning (autoregressive VLM tower) and diffusion-based world/action generation; Nano (16B) and Super (64B) checkpoints, open-source SOTA on R-Bench, leading Artificial Analysis text-to-image and image-to-video.

highlights.title: Unitree clears China STAR Market IPO; named NVIDIA GR00T reference humanoid
highlights.detail: On June 1, 2026 Unitree Robotics cleared the Shanghai Stock Exchange listing-committee review (first 'embodied AI' company approved for China's A-share market, ~$6.2B target, ~4.2B yuan raise), and NVIDIA named Unitree's H2 Plus body as the hardware foundation for its open Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid platform with Jetson Thor compute.

payload.name: Unitree Robotics
payload.why: First embodied-AI company cleared for a China A-share (STAR Market) IPO and chosen as the hardware base for NVIDIA's open Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid; 2025 revenue 1.699B yuan at ~60% gross margins, but carries U.S. national-security/legislative risk.
payload.funding: Targeting ~$6.2B valuation, raising ~4.2B yuan (~$616M) via STAR Market IPO; backers include Meituan, Sequoia China, Tencent, Alibaba, Ant Group

highlights.title: Tesla Robotaxi covers all of Austin metro, but active fleet ~20 vehicles
highlights.detail: On June 3, 2026 Tesla said its unsupervised Robotaxi service now covers the entire Austin metro area, but the active unsupervised fleet has shrunk to ~20 vehicles (peaked at 25 in late April), with Dallas/Houston at 3 and 6. Waymo runs ~3,000 robotaxis and 500K+ paid weekly trips; meaningful ramp is tied to the FSD v15 rewrite (late 2026/early 2027).

payload.field: Tesla Robotaxi Austin status (geofence vs active fleet)
payload.new_value: Entire Austin metro geofence as of June 3, 2026, but only ~20 active unsupervised vehicles (down from 25 late-April peak); Dallas 3, Houston 6; fleet ramp deferred to late 2026/early 2027 pending FSD v15
payload.previous_value: Service limited to part of South Austin with a peak of ~25 cumulative unsupervised vehicles

highlights.title: Intismeran + Keytruda: 5-yr melanoma data, 49% lower recurrence
highlights.detail: At ASCO 2026 (May 29-June 2) Moderna/Merck reported 5-year KEYNOTE-942 data for intismeran autogene (mRNA-4157) plus pembrolizumab: 49% reduction in recurrence/death, 59% reduction in distant metastasis/death, and ~53% OS benefit vs Keytruda alone in resected stage III/IV melanoma. Published simultaneously in JCO.

payload.new_value: 5-year follow-up: 49% reduction in recurrence/death, 59% reduction in distant metastasis/death, ~53% OS benefit vs pembrolizumab monotherapy
payload.previous_value: ~3-year data (~44% RFS benefit) previously reported; 5-year readout not yet available

highlights.title: Amylin analogs emerge as a tolerability-differentiated obesity class
highlights.detail: Roche's June 1, 2026 ADA curtain-raiser unveiled late-breaking Phase 2 ZUPREME-1 data for petrelintide, a long-acting amylin analog positioned for a 'compelling tolerability profile,' alongside enicepatide (CT-388) dual GLP-1/GIP data; Roche is advancing a fixed-dose amylin+incretin combination trial mid-2026.

payload.name: Amylin analogs as next-wave obesity therapeutics
payload.description: Amylin-receptor agonists (e.g. petrelintide) restore leptin sensitivity to induce satiety with markedly better GI tolerability than incretins, enabling co-formulation with GLP-1/GIP agonists like enicepatide.

highlights.title: Mazdutide beats semaglutide head-to-head; GLORY-2 9mg data at ADA
highlights.detail: Innovent's ADA 2026 presentations include the GCG/GLP-1 dual agonist mazdutide's head-to-head DREAMS-3 win over semaglutide (48.0% vs 21.0% achieving HbA1c<7% plus >=10% weight loss) plus Phase 3 GLORY-2 9mg obesity data in Chinese adults.

payload.new_value: 48.0% of mazdutide vs 21.0% of semaglutide patients achieved HbA1c<7.0% plus >=10% weight loss; full data presented ADA 2026
payload.previous_value: DREAMS-3 topline disclosed late 2025; full ADA presentation and GLORY-2 9mg data not yet public

highlights.title: Survodutide SYNCHRONIZE-1 full Phase 3 data at ADA 2026
highlights.detail: Boehringer Ingelheim's glucagon/GLP-1 dual agonist survodutide presented full Phase 3 SYNCHRONIZE-1 data at ADA 2026: up to 16.6% mean weight loss at 76 weeks vs 3.2% placebo, with up to 85.1% achieving >=5% loss.

payload.new_value: Full data at ADA 2026: 16.6% mean weight loss at 76 weeks vs 3.2% placebo; up to 85.1% achieved >=5% reduction
payload.previous_value: Topline only (April 28, 2026 release); full data not yet presented

highlights.title: Ania Jastreboff — lead investigator, retatrutide TRIUMPH-1
highlights.detail: Ania M. Jastreboff (Yale) presented the full pivotal Phase 3 TRIUMPH-1 retatrutide obesity results in the ADA 2026 late-breaking symposium, cementing her as the leading academic investigator across the triple-agonist obesity trials.

payload.name: Ania M. Jastreboff
payload.role: Professor of Medicine; Director, Yale Y-Weight Obesity Research Center; lead investigator, retatrutide TRIUMPH-1

highlights.title: Focused Energy — direct-drive laser fusion startup raises $240M Series A
highlights.detail: Germany/Austin-based Focused Energy closed an oversubscribed $240M Series A (June 2, 2026), bringing total to ~$300M private + $200M grants; lead investor RWE. The direct-drive (no hohlraum) design targets ~10 shots/sec at a decommissioned RWE fission site ('Lighthouse'); NIF target designer Debbie Callahan joined as CSO in Dec 2025.

payload.name: Focused Energy
payload.why: Now one of the most heavily funded fusion startups; inertial-confinement laser fusion using direct-drive targets at high rep-rate, building a 'Lighthouse' demo at a former RWE nuclear site.
payload.funding: $240M Series A (June 2026) led by RWE; ~$300M private + $200M grants total

highlights.title: NVIDIA RTX Spark (N1X): Arm CPU + Blackwell GPU client AI superchip
highlights.detail: At Computex 2026 (May 31-June 2) NVIDIA unveiled the RTX Spark Superchip (N1X), a Windows-on-Arm platform pairing an Arm CPU with a Blackwell GPU and up to 128GB unified memory, claiming ~1 petaflop AI and the ability to run 120B-parameter LLMs locally with up to 1M-token context. Ships fall 2026 in laptops/mini-desktops from Microsoft, Dell, HP, ASUS, Lenovo, MSI.

payload.name: NVIDIA RTX Spark (N1X) client AI superchip
payload.description: Arm-CPU + Blackwell-GPU SoC with 128GB unified memory bringing data-center-class local AI inference (120B-param LLMs, 1M-token context) to consumer Windows PCs.
examples: Windows-on-Arm agentic AI laptops from Dell/HP/Microsoft/ASUS/Lenovo/MSI

highlights.title: SpaceX drone ship OCISLY notches 200th booster catch; B1088 flies 16th
highlights.detail: On June 3, 2026 (Starlink 17-47 from Vandenberg), the drone ship 'Of Course I Still Love You' reached its 200th successful booster landing — the first drone ship ever to 200 — marking SpaceX's 619th total booster recovery. Booster B1088 flew its 16th mission.

payload.field: SpaceX total booster recoveries / single-drone-ship landings
payload.previous_value: 617th total recovery as of May 30, 2026 (OCISLY at 199)

highlights.title: Blue Origin targets New Glenn return-to-flight before end of 2026
highlights.detail: Following the May 28 static-fire explosion that wrecked the Cape Canaveral pad, Blue Origin CEO Dave Limp said (June 2-3, 2026) the company aims to fly New Glenn again before year's end, reporting pad infrastructure, a flown booster and three upper stages 'look good' — far more aggressive than industry assumptions of 2027+.

payload.field: Blue Origin New Glenn return-to-flight target
payload.new_value: Before end of 2026 (per CEO Dave Limp, June 2026)
payload.previous_value: Indeterminate after May 28 static-fire explosion (industry est. 2027+)

highlights.title: CATL confirms sodium-ion manufacturing solved, mass production in 2026
highlights.detail: On May 30, 2026, CATL chief scientist Wu Kai confirmed manufacturing bottlenecks are resolved and a series of mass-produced Naxtra sodium-ion products will ship in 2026 across passenger EVs, fleets, swapping networks and stationary storage. First passenger EV (Changan Nevo A06) launches mid-2026; cells at 175 Wh/kg, 400+ km range, operating to -40C.

payload.new_value: Manufacturing bottlenecks solved; mass production confirmed for 2026 (May 30, 2026)
payload.previous_value: Announced/sampling with mass production planned but unconfirmed

highlights.title: Lean-4 proof of when JEPA world models recover true latents
highlights.detail: LeCun, David Klindt (Cold Spring Harbor) and Randall Balestriero (Brown) posted 'When Does LeJEPA Learn a World Model?' (arXiv 2605.26379, submitted May 25, 2026), proving in Lean 4 the exact conditions (isotropic-Gaussian latents, stationary additive-noise dynamics) under which LeJEPA linearly recovers a world's true hidden variables, with a paired benchmark showing current models remain brittle outside those conditions.

payload.claim: Self-supervised world-model learning now has a machine-checkable (Lean 4) identifiability guarantee: LeJEPA provably recovers a world's true latent variables iff the latents are Gaussian and evolve under stationary additive-noise dynamics (arXiv 2605.26379, May 2026).

highlights.title: David Klindt — formal-identifiability theorist for world models
highlights.detail: Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory researcher who co-authored (with LeCun and Balestriero) the viral May 2026 Lean-4-formalized proof on when JEPA-style world models recover true latents, releasing accompanying identifiability simulations and proofs on GitHub.

payload.name: David Klindt
payload.focus: Identifiability theory and formal (Lean 4) guarantees for self-supervised / world-model representation learning
payload.notes: Lead theorist behind 'When Does LeJEPA Learn a World Model?' (arXiv 2605.26379), bringing machine-checked proofs into the world-models debate. A formal-rigor counterweight to the world-models hype cycle.

highlights.title: Context engineering and evals are the new agent bottleneck
highlights.detail: Multiple independent sources argue the limiting factor for agentic AI has shifted from model capability to (a) supplying the right context at the right time and (b) the cost/latency of evaluation, reframing 'eval engineering' and 'context engineering' as first-class disciplines.

payload.thesis: With frontier models 'good enough,' the binding constraint on agentic systems is no longer the model but the surrounding engineering: getting correct context to the right agent at the right time, and the compute/latency cost of running evals and validators to govern agent behavior.
examples: The New Stack: 'Context is AI coding's real bottleneck in 2026'
Hugging Face: 'AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck'
SiliconANGLE: 'Eval engineering: the missing piece of agentic AI governance'

highlights.title: Deep Fission's mile-deep borehole SMR enters construction
highlights.detail: Day & Zimmermann and Deep Fission announced a June 2, 2026 partnership to build the 'Gravity' pressurized-water SMR one mile underground in a borehole, with first criticality targeted for July 4, 2026 in Parsons, Kansas. Deep-borehole siting uses rock overburden for passive containment — a distinct architecture from surface SMRs.

payload.why: A fundamentally different SMR deployment model (1-mile-deep boreholes for passive containment and rapid siting) moving from design to construction with a near-term criticality target.

highlights.title: Growth-factor-loaded cellulose scaffolds cut cultivated-meat cost ~10x
highlights.detail: Hebrew University of Jerusalem researchers (reported June 2026) bound growth factors directly onto directionally-frozen plant-cellulose scaffolds rather than dispersing them in media, supporting bovine stem-cell growth with up to 10x fewer expensive factors and yielding pan-fried constructs with sirloin-like texture.

payload.candidate_area: cultivated meat / cellular agriculture cost reduction
payload.why: Growth-factor cost is the dominant barrier to cultivated-meat economics; a scaffold-binding approach giving ~10x factor efficiency plus realistic texture attacks the core unit-economics problem.
